analysis.mechanism: Reduces navigation uncertainty by making the path legible, which lowers cognitive load and action paralysis.
analysis.mechanism_source: Sweller, 1988, Cognitive Load During Problem Solving
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Removes performance pressure by stating that movement is penalty-free, supporting autonomy under low threat.
analysis.mechanism_source: Deci & Ryan, 2000, Self-Determination Theory
analysis.current_issue: EN, zh-TW, zh-CN, and ja all add a directive equivalent to 'Just move,' which drifts from permission into advice.

en.interface: There is no fail state here. You can keep moving without penalty.

zh-TW.interface: 這裡沒有失敗狀態。你可以持續移動而不受懲罰。

zh-CN.interface: 这里没有失败状态。你可以持续移动而不受惩罚。

ja.interface: ここに失敗状態はありません。ペナルティなく動き続けられます。

analysis.mechanism: Stable timing contingencies increase perceived control by making success conditions predictable across attempts.
analysis.mechanism_source: Skinner, 1996, A Guide to Constructs of Control
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Defines a valid stopping boundary, using manageable subgoals to reduce session burden and support perceived progress.
analysis.mechanism_source: Amabile & Kramer, 2011, The Progress Principle
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Immediate feedback strengthens action-outcome mapping, which supports perceived competence and rapid calibration.
analysis.mechanism_source: Bandura, 1997, Self-Efficacy
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Constrains the problem space and names the control tool, lowering cognitive load under environmental threat.
analysis.mechanism_source: Sweller, 1988, Cognitive Load During Problem Solving
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Frames choices as directional rather than binding, preserving autonomy while reducing commitment pressure.
analysis.mechanism_source: Deci & Ryan, 2000, Self-Determination Theory
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: De-emphasizes completion goals, reducing completionist pressure by making open-ended wandering valid.
analysis.mechanism_source: Deci & Ryan, 2000, Self-Determination Theory
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve permission semantics; the wording remains optional rather than directive.

analysis.mechanism: Reframes the task from analysis to observation, lowering evaluative load and cognitive demand.
analysis.mechanism_source: Sweller, 1988, Cognitive Load During Problem Solving
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Normalizes concealment as core play, shifting threat appraisal from personal inadequacy to environmental design.
analysis.mechanism_source: Lazarus & Folkman, 1984, Stress, Appraisal, and Coping
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Reframes death as systemic progression rather than personal failure, reducing self-evaluative threat.
analysis.mechanism_source: Dweck, 2006, Mindset
analysis.current_issue: EN kernel anthropomorphizes the loop as expecting the player, and zh-TW/zh-CN use 退步 for setback/skill regression; all four can be made more purely descriptive.

en.interface: Death is part of progress here. You are entering a reset loop, not a skill loss.

zh-TW.interface: 死亡是進度的一部分。你進入的是重置循環，不是能力損失。

zh-CN.interface: 死亡是进度的一部分。你进入的是重置循环，不是能力损失。

ja.kernel: サイクルは想定内です。
ja.interface: 死は進行の一部です。あなたが入るのはリセットのループであり、能力の損失ではありません。

analysis.mechanism: Invokes automaticity, reducing working-memory demands by shifting control to practiced sensorimotor routines.
analysis.mechanism_source: Schneider & Shiffrin, 1977, Controlled and Automatic Human Information Processing
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Separates defeat from identity evaluation, reducing ego threat and protecting persistence after failure.
analysis.mechanism_source: Dweck, 2006, Mindset
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Eliminates time pressure, increasing self-pacing and reducing urgency-driven cognitive strain.
analysis.mechanism_source: Sweller, 1988, Cognitive Load During Problem Solving
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Observer framing reduces problem-solving obligation by shifting the role from solver to witness.
analysis.mechanism_source: Sweller, 1988, Cognitive Load During Problem Solving
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Offloads maintenance to an automated system, reducing felt obligation and permitting shorter engagement windows.
analysis.mechanism_source: Parasuraman, Sheridan, & Wickens, 2000, A Model for Types and Levels of Human Interaction with Automation
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations use reassurance ('will be fine') and sufficiency language ('enough'), which drifts from descriptive permission into comfort/advice.

en.interface: Crops continue on their own. You can stop after a short in-game day.

zh-TW.interface: 作物會自行生長。你可以在短短一天後結束。

zh-CN.interface: 作物会自行生长。你可以在短短一天后结束。

ja.interface: 作物は自動で育ちます。ゲーム内の一日が短くても、そこで止められます。

analysis.mechanism: Rhythmic alternation increases temporal predictability, supporting perceived control in high-intensity combat.
analysis.mechanism_source: Skinner, 1996, A Guide to Constructs of Control
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Removing time limits reduces threat appraisal and allows self-paced problem engagement.
analysis.mechanism_source: Lazarus & Folkman, 1984, Stress, Appraisal, and Coping
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations personify the puzzle as waiting, which adds reassurance rather than staying purely descriptive.

en.interface: There is no time limit here. You can stay with one puzzle as long as you want.

zh-TW.interface: 這裡沒有時間限制。你可以在同一個謎題前停留多久都可以。

zh-CN.interface: 这里没有时间限制。你可以在同一个谜题前停留多久都可以。

ja.interface: ここに時間制限はありません。同じパズルにいくらでも留まれます。

analysis.mechanism: Narrows the active sensory channel, reducing cross-modal competition and simplifying attention allocation.
analysis.mechanism_source: Sweller, 1988, Cognitive Load During Problem Solving
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Defines a discrete unit of progress, which reduces burden by making survival measurable one day at a time.
analysis.mechanism_source: Amabile & Kramer, 2011, The Progress Principle
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Slow, controllable systems reduce urgency and support low-pressure monitoring through perceived control.
analysis.mechanism_source: Skinner, 1996, A Guide to Constructs of Control
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations add advisory/reassuring language ('No rush') and a commanding formulation ('at your command' / 按你的指令), which is less purely descriptive.

en.interface: Water systems respond slowly. You can leave them running without constant input.

zh-TW.interface: 水系統的反應偏慢。你可以讓它持續運作，不必一直輸入。

zh-CN.interface: 水系统的反应偏慢。你可以让它持续运作，不必一直操作。

ja.interface: 水系の反応は緩やかです。常時入力しなくても、そのまま動かせます。

analysis.mechanism: Low-stakes framing reduces evaluative anxiety by describing service as the core loop without added pressure.
analysis.mechanism_source: Lazarus & Folkman, 1984, Stress, Appraisal, and Coping
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.

analysis.mechanism: Permanent local gains leverage the progress principle, making small actions feel cumulatively meaningful.
analysis.mechanism_source: Amabile & Kramer, 2011, The Progress Principle
analysis.current_issue: EN and all translations preserve descriptive permission semantics; no advice, encouragement, or judgment drift.
